sourceMovie.title: Automata
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Automata (2014) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Gabe Ibáñez's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Blade Runner
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Automata clicked for you, Blade Runner (1982) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Automata?
faq.answer: Try Blade Runner, The Terminator, Looper—each shares DNA with Automata while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Automata?
faq.answer: Automata (2014) is best described as Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Automata worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
